concerns.text: FR-003 requires the system to fit a mixed-effects model with random effects (1|participant_id) IF the data is within-subjects. The plan explicitly restricts the design to 'Between-Subjects' and omits mixed-effects logic. Tasks T020-T023 only implement a Fixed-Effects Linear Model (ANOVA). There is no task to implement the conditional logic or the mixed-effects model required by FR-003 for the within-subjects edge case.

FR-002 mandates a binning strategy or flagging mechanism if input data has more than two levels for status/behavior. Tasks T010-T014 assume the synthetic generator produces exactly two levels and only check for zero variance (T011). No task exists to implement the binning logic or ambiguity flagging required if external data were ingested.

The spec's Edge Cases require detecting continuous vs. binary outcome variables and switching regression families (Gaussian vs. Binomial). Tasks T020-T023 describe implementing a 'Fixed-Effects Linear Model' (implying Gaussian) without a specific task to detect outcome type and dynamically switch to logistic regression if the data is binary.

FR-005 defines the outlier sweep based on 'absolute deviation from the *cell mean* within each of the 4 experimental conditions'. Task T030 is vague ('Sensitivity analysis module to sweep outlier exclusion threshold') and does not explicitly mandate calculating deviations relative to the specific 4-condition cell means, risking implementation against a global mean which violates the spec.

Success Criteria SC-001 and SC-002 require measuring statistical significance (p < 0.05) against the null hypothesis. The plan redefines the project as a 'Parameter Recovery Study' measuring 'Recovery Accuracy' against injected parameters. No task explicitly implements the hypothesis testing logic (p-value calculation against zero) required to satisfy SC-001/SC-002, creating a gap between the spec's success criteria and the planned implementation.
